title: Anders Herfindahl-Quint
text:
Hello! I am a senior chemistry major who enjoys learning new things and problem solving.

1 + 1

[1] 2

title: Project 1
text:
The NYTimes dataset contains text of all the titles for articles from 1996 - 2006. I want to look at how the sentiment, topics, and word choice changes over the years.


Joining with `by = join_by(word)`
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'year', 'month'. You can override using the
`.groups` argument.









Most months have an overall negative sentiment score (greater than %50). There are varying number of articles per month so some months only have a few data points while others have a lot more. Some notable points are May 1997, February 1998, November 2002 and 2003 all of which have 100% negative sentiment. These coincide with natural disasters and terrorist attacks being the only reported on news in the data base.









The most common topics change over the years. Up until 2001 the Federal Government was a large part of the news while Defense was a much smaller part. After and during 2001 Defense because a much larger part of the news. This coincides with 9/11 and the war in Iraq. The dataset ends at 2006 which is still during the war in Iraq so Defense is still a large part of the news. International Affairs has always been a large part of the news which makes sense as the NYT is not a solely domestic news source.


Joining with `by = join_by(word)`









Up until 2001 a majority of the news was focused on politics with words like “campaign” and “politics” as well as names like “clinton” and “dole” being the most commonly used words. Same as before, after 2001 Iraq becomes a huge part of the news. Words like “Iraq” and “war” become huge parts of the news and politics becomes a lesser part of the news.

library(usdata);

states_sf <- 
  read_sf("https://rstudio.github.io/leaflet/json/us-states.geojson") |>
  select(name, geometry)

states_sf <- states_sf |>
  mutate(name = str_to_lower(str_squish(name))) |>
  filter(!(str_detect(name, "alaska|hawaii")))


states_map <- state_stats |> 
  mutate(region = tolower(state)) |>
  select(region, unempl, poverty) |>
  inner_join(map_data("state"))

Joining with `by = join_by(region)`

int_states_map <- states_sf |> 
  inner_join(state_stats |> 
    mutate(region = tolower(state)) |>
    select(region, unempl, poverty),
  by =c("name" = "region"))


bins <- c(0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, Inf)
pal <- colorBin("YlOrRd", domain = int_states_map, bins = bins)

int_states_map <- int_states_map |>
  mutate(labels = str_c(name, ": ", unempl, " % Unemployed"))

labels <- lapply(int_states_map$labels, HTML)


ggplot(states_map, aes(map_id = region)) +
  geom_map(aes(fill = unempl), map = states_map) +
  expand_limits(x = states_map$long, y = states_map$lat) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c() +
  theme_void() +
  labs(x = "", y = "", fill = "Unemployment (%)")








All data aquired from the usdata package

leaflet(int_states_map) |>
  setView(-96, 37.8, 4) |>
  addTiles() |>
  addPolygons(
    fillColor = ~pal(unempl),
    weight = 3,
    opacity = 1,
    color = "white",
    dashArray = "4",
    fillOpacity = 0.7,
    highlightOptions = highlightOptions(
      weight = 5,
      color = "#000",
      dashArray = "",
      fillOpacity = 0.7,
      bringToFront = TRUE),
    label = labels,
    labelOptions = labelOptions(
      style = list("font-weight" = "bold", padding = "3px 8px"),
      textsize = "15px",
      direction = "auto")) |>
  addLegend(pal = pal, values = ~unempl, opacity = 0.7, title = NULL,
    position = "bottomright")
